sections.section: The Night the Earth Didn't Sleep

tree.text: The Night the Earth Didn't Sleep
tree.notes: 关于1976年唐山大地震的纪实性文章，包含震前兆头、震时惨状、震后救援与重建。
tree.statement: 本文以时间为线索，叙述了唐山大地震的前后过程。
tree.explanation: 文章按震前异象、地震爆发、毁灭性损坏、救援工作和新唐山建立的顺序进行了清晰的叙述。

children.text: Paragraph 1: Warning Signs (震前异象)
children.cn: 第一段：震前的种种怪异前兆
children.notes: 地震发生前几天自然界和动物的异常反应。
children.statement: 本段通过描写水井和多种动物的异动来暗示地震的前兆。
children.explanation: 段落中详细叙述了井水升降、臭气和鸡、猪、狗、老鼠及鱼的恐慌行为。

children.text: Well & Water Anomalies (水井及气体异变)
Strange things were happening in the countryside of northeastern Hebei.
children.cn: 在河北东北部的农村地区，一些奇怪的事情正在发生。
children.notes: were happening 是过去进行时，表示过去某段时间内正在发生的情况。
children.statement: countryside 意为“乡村，农村地区”。
children.explanation: countryside 指城市的郊区或广大农村地区。
children.highlight: were happening, in the

children.text: For several days, the water in the village wells rose and fell, rose and fell.
children.cn: 几天来，村里井里的水升了又降，降了又升。
children.notes: rose 和 fell 分别是 rise 和 fall 的过去式；这里重复动词起强调作用。
children.statement: rose 是 raise 的过去式。
children.explanation: rose 是不及物动词 rise 的过去式，而 raise 的过去式是 raised。
children.highlight: For several days, rose and fell

children.text: There were deep cracks that appeared in the well walls.
children.cn: 井壁上出现了深深的裂缝。
children.notes: that appeared in the well walls 是定语从句，修饰先行词 cracks。
children.statement: that 在定语从句中作主语，可以省略。
children.explanation: 关系代词在定语从句中作主语时不能省略，作宾语时通常可以省略。
children.highlight: cracks that appeared

children.text: At least one well had some smelly gas coming out of it.
children.cn: 至少有一口井里有难闻的气体冒出来。
children.notes: coming out of it 是现在分词短语作宾语补足语，表示状态或动作进行。
children.statement: smelly 是动词，意思是“闻起来”。
children.explanation: smelly 是形容词，意思是“有难闻气味的，臭的”。
children.keywords: at least, smelly gas, coming out
children.highlight: had some, coming out of

children.text: Animal Panic & Panic Behavior (动物异常与恐慌)
Chickens and even pigs were too nervous to eat, and dogs refused to go inside buildings.
children.cn: 鸡甚至猪都紧张得不吃东西，狗也拒绝进到屋里去。
children.notes: too ... to ... 意为“太……而不能”；refuse 后接动词不定式 to do sth.。
children.statement: refused to go inside buildings 中的 to go 是不定式作宾语。
children.explanation: refuse 后面跟 to do 不定式作宾语，表示拒绝做某事。
children.highlight: too nervous to, refused to

children.text: Mice ran out of the fields looking for places to hide, and fish jumped out of the water.
children.cn: 老鼠从田里跑出来寻找藏身之处，鱼儿也跳出了水面。
children.notes: looking for 是现在分词短语作伴随状语；jump out of 意为“跳出”。
children.statement: Mice 是 mouse 的复数形式。
children.explanation: mouse 的复数形式是不规则变化 mice。
children.highlight: ran out of, looking for, out of

children.text: Paragraph 2: The Moment of Quake (地震降临)
children.cn: 第二段：震魔降临的一瞬间
children.notes: 大地震发生的精确时间以及毁灭性后果。
children.statement: 本段记叙了在人们熟睡的凌晨，毁灭性的地震在数秒内摧毁了唐山。
children.explanation: 段落记录了3点的大光，3:42的大地震动，以及一分钟内城市化为废墟的场景。

children.text: Signs & Premonition (大震前的兆头与沉睡的市民)
At about 3:00 a.m., on 28 July 1976, bright lights were seen in the sky outside the city of Tangshan and loud noises were heard.
children.cn: 1976年7月28日凌晨3点左右，唐山市外的天空中出现了耀眼的光芒，并听到了巨大的声响。
children.notes: were seen 和 were heard 都是过去时被动语态，表示动作承受者作主语。
children.statement: sky 前必须加上冠词 the。
children.explanation: 独一无二的事物，如 sky, sun, moon 等前面常加定冠词 the。
children.keywords: bright lights, sky, Tangshan, loud noises
children.highlight: were seen in, were heard

children.text: But the city's one million people were asleep as usual that night.
children.cn: 但那天晚上，这座城市的一百万居民像往常一样沉睡着。
children.notes: were asleep 作为系表结构，表示睡着的状态；as usual 意为“像往常一样”。
children.statement: asleep 可以作定语修饰名词，例如 an asleep baby。
children.explanation: asleep 是表语形容词，通常只能作表语，不能作前置定语。作定语表示“睡着的”一般用 sleeping。
children.highlight: were asleep as usual

children.text: Earthquake Outbreak & Impact (大震爆发与波及全国)
At 3:42 a.m., everything began to shake.
children.cn: 凌晨3点42分，一切都开始剧烈摇晃。
children.notes: began 是 begin 的过去式；shake 意为“摇晃，颤抖”。
children.statement: shake 的过去式是不规则动词 shook。
children.explanation: begin - began - begun ； shake - shook - shaken。
children.highlight: began to shake

children.text: It seemed as if the world were coming to an end!
children.cn: 世界似乎就要走到尽头了！
children.notes: It seemed as if 引导表语从句，由于是不真实假设，从句中使用虚拟语气 were coming。
children.statement: come to an end 是固定短语，表示“结束”。
children.explanation: come to an end 表示终结，通常不使用被动语态。
children.keywords: seemed, as if, world, coming to an end
children.highlight: It seemed as if, were coming to

children.text: Eleven kilometres directly below the city, one of the most deadly earthquakes of the 20th century had begun, a quake that even caused damage more than 150 kilometres away in Beijing.
children.cn: 在城市下方11公里的深处，20世纪最致命的一场地震已经开始，这场大震甚至对150多公里外的北京造成了破坏。
children.notes: had begun 是过去完成时；that even caused... 是定语从句，修饰 a quake。
children.statement: that 在从句中修饰 a quake，不能换成 who。
children.explanation: 先行词 a quake 是物，因此只能用 that 或 which 修饰，不能用 who。
children.highlight: one of the, had begun, a quake that

children.text: Nearly one third of the whole nation felt it!
children.cn: 全国将近三分之一的人都感觉到了震动！
children.notes: one third 表示“三分之一”，分子用基数词，分母用序数词。
children.statement: one third 中 third 必须用复数形式 thirds。
children.explanation: 分子是 1 时，分母用单数序数词；分子大于 1 时（如 two thirds），分母的序数词才用复数形式。
children.highlight: one third of, felt it

children.text: Immediate Physical Ruins (瞬间的山河改观与城市废墟)
A huge crack, eight kilometres long and 30 metres wide, cut across houses, roads, and waterways.
children.cn: 一条长8公里、宽30米的巨大裂缝横穿了房屋、道路和水道。
children.notes: long 和 wide 作后置定语修饰 crack 的尺寸；cut across 意为“横穿，横跨”。
children.statement: cut 是过去式，其原形、过去式和过去分词同形。
children.explanation: 动词 cut 是不规则动词，变化形式为 cut - cut - cut。
children.keywords: huge crack, kilometres long, cut across
children.highlight: long and, wide, cut across

children.text: Hard hills of rock became rivers of dirt.
children.cn: 坚硬的石山变成了滚滚泥流。
children.notes: dirt 意为“泥土，污垢”；became 是 become 的过去式。
children.statement: rock 意为“岩石”，在此处是不可数名词表示岩石材质。
children.explanation: rock 作“岩石，石块”的材质讲时是不可数名词，作具体的“石块”时是可数名词。
children.keywords: hard hills, rock, became, rivers of dirt
children.highlight: became, of

children.text: In less than one minute, a large city lay in ruins.
children.cn: 在不到一分钟的时间里，一座大城市化为废墟。
children.notes: lay 是 lie 的过去式，表示“处于某种状态”；in ruins 意为“一片废墟，严重受损”。
children.statement: lay 是及物动词，在此处表示“放置”。
children.explanation: 此处的 lay 是不及物动词 lie（处于某种状态，平躺）的过去式，其变形为 lie - lay - lain。
children.keywords: less than, minute, lay in ruins
children.highlight: In less than, lay in ruins

children.text: Paragraph 3: Widespread Damage (满目疮痍)
children.cn: 第三段：严重的财产和人员损失
children.notes: 地震发生后唐山所蒙受的巨大死伤与设施彻底瘫痪。
children.statement: 本段列举了大量具体百分比和伤亡数字，以客观真实地展现地震破坏力。
children.explanation: 本段发掘了400,000人死伤，75%工厂，90%家园，以及全部医院被毁的数据。

children.text: Human Casualties & Orphaning (人员巨大伤亡与孤儿)
Two thirds of the people who lived there were dead or injured.
children.cn: 居住在那里的人有三分之二死亡或受伤。
children.notes: two thirds 三分之二；who lived there 是定语从句修饰 people。
children.statement: who 在从句中作主语，可以用 whom 代替。
children.explanation: whom 只能在定语从句中作宾语，作主语必须用 who 或者是 that。
children.highlight: Two thirds of, who lived, were dead or

children.text: Thousands of children were left without parents.
children.cn: 成千上万的孩子失去了父母。
children.notes: thousands of 意为“数以千计的，成千上万的”；without parents 意为“没有父母”。
children.statement: thousands of 前面如果有数字，也必须加 s，例如 two thousands of。
children.explanation: 如果有具体数字修饰，应使用 thousand 的单数形式且不加 of，例如 two thousand children。数以千计用 thousands of 结构。
children.highlight: Thousands of, left without

children.text: The number of people who were killed or badly injured in the quake was more than 400,000.
children.cn: 在地震中死亡或重伤的人数超过了40万。
children.notes: the number of ... 作主语，其谓语动词用单数形式 was；more than 意为“超过，多于”。
children.statement: who were killed or badly injured 中的 badly 是副词修饰形容词/分词 injured。
children.explanation: badly 是副词，意为“严重地”，在此处修饰动词过去分词 injured，起强调程度的作用。
children.highlight: The number of, who were, was more than

children.text: Infrastructure & Building Damage (厂房医院等建筑毁灭)
Everywhere survivors looked, there was nothing but ruins.
children.cn: 幸存者无论看向哪里，都只有废墟。
children.notes: nothing but 意为“仅仅，只不过，除了……什么也没有”。
children.statement: nothing but 相当于 only 表达的意思。
children.explanation: nothing but ruins 意思是除了废墟别无他物，也就是“只有废墟”。
children.highlight: looked, there was, nothing but

children.text: Nearly everything in the city was destroyed.
children.cn: 这座城市里几乎所有的东西都被摧毁了。
children.notes: Nearly 意为“几乎”；was destroyed 是过去时被动语态。
children.statement: everything 作主语时，谓语动词用复数。
children.explanation: 不定代词 everything, something, everyone 等作主语时，谓语动词都用单数形式。
children.highlight: Nearly, in the city, was destroyed

children.text: About 75 percent of the city's factories and buildings, 90 percent of its homes, and all of its hospitals were gone.
children.cn: 全市大约75%的工厂 and 建筑物，90%的家园，以及所有的医院都消失了。
children.notes: percent 意为“百分之……”；were gone 意为“消失了，没有了”。
children.statement: percent 没有复数形式，其后不加 s。
children.explanation: percent 是一个固定词汇，不论百分比数值大小，形式均不变，不加 s。
children.highlight: About, percent of the, and all of, were gone

children.text: Bricks covered the ground like red autumn leaves, but no wind could blow them away.
children.cn: 砖块覆盖着地面，就像红色的秋叶，但风却吹不走它们。
children.notes: covered... like... 像……一样盖住了；blow away 意为“吹走”。
children.statement: blow them away 中的 them 必须放在 blow 和 away 中间，不能写成 blow away them。
children.explanation: 在“动词 + 副词”短语中，当代词作宾语时，代词必须放在动词和副词的中间。
children.keywords: bricks, covered, autumn leaves, blow away
children.highlight: covered the, like, but, blow them away

children.text: Bridges & Railway Destruction (桥梁坍塌与铁轨瘫痪)
Most bridges had fallen or were not safe to cross.
children.cn: 大多数桥梁已经坍塌，或者很不安全，无法跨越。
children.notes: had fallen 是过去完成时，表示在过去发生地震的那一时刻，桥梁已经塌了；safe to cross 跨越很安全。
children.statement: cross 是名词，意为“十字架”。
children.explanation: cross 在此句中是动词，意为“跨越，横穿”。
children.highlight: Most, had fallen, or were not, to cross

children.text: The railway tracks were now useless pieces of metal.
children.cn: 铁轨现在成了没用的金属片。
children.notes: useless 意为“无用的”，是 useful 的反义词；pieces of metal 金属片。
children.statement: useless 中的 -less 是一个否定名词后缀。
children.explanation: -less 是一个否定形容词后缀，加在名词后构成形容词，表示“没有……的”。
children.highlight: were now, pieces of

children.text: Livestock Death (牲畜惨重死伤)
Tens of thousands of cows, hundreds of thousands of pigs, and millions of chickens were dead.
children.cn: 数以万计的奶牛、数十万头猪和数百万只鸡都死去了。
children.notes: tens of thousands of 数以万计；hundreds of thousands of 数十万；millions of 数百万。
children.statement: cows 和 pigs 都是可数名词复数。
children.explanation: cow (奶牛) 和 pig (猪) 均为规则可数名词，复数加 s。
children.keywords: tens of thousands, cows, pigs, millions, dead
children.highlight: Tens of thousands of, and millions of, were dead

children.text: Subsequent Quake & Shortages (二次强震与物资匮乏)
Sand now filled the wells instead of water.
children.cn: 沙子现在填满了水井，而不是水。
children.notes: filled ... instead of ... 填满了……而不是……。
children.statement: instead of 是介词短语，后接名词、代词或动名词。
children.explanation: instead of 意为“代替，而不是”，作为介词短语后面可以接名词 water。
children.highlight: filled the, instead of

children.text: People were in shock—and then, later that afternoon, another big quake shook Tangshan again.
children.cn: 人们惊魂未定——接着，那天下午晚些时候，另一场大地震再次震动了唐山。
children.notes: in shock 处于震惊中；shook 是 shake 的过去式。
children.statement: in shock 是动词短语。
children.explanation: in shock 是介词短语，作表语表示人们所处的状态。
children.highlight: People were in, and then, another, again

children.text: Even more buildings fell down.
children.cn: 甚至更多的建筑物倒塌了。
children.notes: fell down 是 fall down 的过去式，表示倒塌。
children.statement: fell 是 fall 的过去式形式。
children.explanation: fall 的过去式是 fell，过去分词是 fallen。
children.highlight: Even more, fell down

children.text: Water, food, and electricity were hard to get.
children.cn: 水、食物和电都很难得到。
children.notes: were hard to get 难以得到，不定式 active form 表示被动含义。
children.statement: electricity 是可数名词。
children.explanation: electricity（电）是不可数名词，没有复数形式。
children.keywords: water, food, electricity, hard to get
children.highlight: were hard to

children.text: People began to wonder how long the disaster would last.
children.cn: 人们开始纳闷这场灾难会持续多久。
children.notes: began to wonder 开始纳闷；how long ... would last 宾语从句作 wonder 宾语。
children.statement: would last 中的 last 是动词，意思是“持续”。
children.explanation: last 作为动词时，意为“持续，维持”。
children.keywords: began, wonder, how long, disaster, last
children.highlight: began to, how long the, would last

children.text: Paragraph 4: Relief and Assistance (救援与希望)
children.cn: 第四段：灾后各方的迅速支援与救援行动
children.notes: 政府和军队迅速派员抗震救灾，全国各地运送物资送去希望。
children.statement: 本段描述了国家力量的介入使得唐山这座城市重新恢复了生命力。
children.explanation: 本段详细叙述了派兵挖掘被困人员，提供医疗服务，建立临时避难所及运送食物和水的救援努力。

children.text: Military Rescue Efforts (军队救灾与遗体清理)
But hope was not lost.
children.cn: 但希望并没有破灭。
children.notes: hope was not lost 采用了过去时的被动语态形式。
children.statement: lost 是 lose 的过去分词形式，在此起形容词作用，意为“失去的，消失的”。
children.explanation: lose - lost - lost，此处作表语表示状态。
children.highlight: But, was not

children.text: Soon after the quakes, the army sent 150,000 soldiers to Tangshan to dig out those who were trapped and to bury the dead.
children.cn: 地震后不久，军队派了15万名士兵到唐山，挖出那些被困的人并掩埋死难者。
children.notes: to dig out ... and to bury ... 两个不定式作目的状语；those who were trapped 限制性定语从句。
children.statement: the dead 在这里表示复数概念，指的是“死难者们”。
children.explanation: “the + 形容词”结构，如 the dead, the rich, the poor 等可以表示一类人，作主语时谓语动词用复数形式。
children.keywords: army, sent soldiers, dig out, trapped, bury the dead
children.highlight: Soon after the, to dig out, those who were, and to bury the

children.text: Medical Care Support (医护人员驰援)
More than 10,000 doctors and nurses came to provide medical care.
children.cn: 一万多名医生和护士前来提供医疗救助。
children.notes: provide medical care 提供医疗照料；came to do sth. 来做某事。
children.statement: provide 常用结构是 provide sb. with sth. 或 provide sth. for sb.。
children.explanation: provide 的搭配主要为 provide sth. for/to sb. 或 provide sb. with sth.。
children.highlight: More than, came to

children.text: Shelter Rebuilding & Help (搭建庇护所与难民得助)
Workers built shelters for survivors whose homes had been destroyed.
children.cn: 工人们为家园被毁的幸存者建造了避难所。
children.notes: whose homes had been destroyed 是定语从句，whose 是关系代词表示所属关系。
children.statement: whose 引导的定语从句，其先行词是 survivors。
children.explanation: whose 修饰 homes，表示“幸存者们的家园”，先行词为 survivors。
children.keywords: workers, built shelters, survivors, whose homes, destroyed
children.highlight: built shelters for, whose homes had been

children.text: Hundreds of thousands of people were helped.
children.cn: 数十万人得到了帮助。
children.notes: hundreds of thousands of 数以十万计；were helped 过去时被动语态。
children.statement: people 作为“人”讲时，单复数同形，本身是复数名词。
children.explanation: people 表示“人们”时是集体名词，看作复数，谓语动词用 were。
children.highlight: Hundreds of thousands of, were helped

children.text: Lifeline Logistics & Revival (生命线输送与城市复苏)
Water and food were brought into the city by train, truck, and plane.
children.cn: 水和食物通过火车、卡车和飞机被源源不断地运进城市。
children.notes: were brought 是 bring 的过去式被动语态；by train, truck, and plane 表示运输工具，不加冠词。
children.statement: by train, truck, and plane 中，交通工具应该加 a 或 the，如 by a train。
children.explanation: “by + 交通工具单数名词”表示出行或运输方式时，中间不能使用任何冠词，如 by train, by bus。
children.keywords: water, food, brought, train, truck, plane
children.highlight: were brought into, by, and

children.text: Slowly, the city began to breathe again.
children.cn: 慢慢地，这座城市又开始呼吸了。
children.notes: Slowly 副词修饰整句；began to breathe 开始呼吸。
children.statement: breathe 是名词，意为“呼吸”。
children.explanation: breathe 是动词形式，其名词形式是 breath (不带词尾e)。
children.highlight: Slowly, the, began to, again

children.text: Paragraph 5: Revival and Future (城市重生)
children.cn: 第五段：唐山的重建、振兴与不屈的精神
children.notes: 在政府支持和全国人民的努力下，唐山从废墟中崛起，向世界证明了人类的力量。
children.statement: 文章的结尾升华了主题，突出了团结协作和乐观面对灾难的重要智慧。
children.explanation: 段落总结了新唐山的建立、人口的增长以及体现出的共同奋斗、团结和 stay positive 的核心智慧。

children.text: New Tangshan Rebuilding (重建新唐山与不懈努力)
Tangshan started to revive itself and get back up on its feet.
children.cn: 唐山开始重新振兴，重新站稳脚跟。
children.notes: revive itself 使自己复兴；get back up on one's feet 重新站起来，恢复原状。
children.statement: itself 是反身代词，指代 Tangshan。
children.explanation: 主语和动词的宾语指的是同一个对象时，宾语必须使用反身代词本身（itself）。
children.keywords: revive, itself, get back up, feet
children.highlight: started to, itself and, get back up on

children.text: With strong support from the government and the tireless efforts of the city's people, a new Tangshan was built upon the earthquake ruins.
children.cn: 在政府的强力支持和全市人民的不懈努力下，一座崭新的唐山在地震废墟上建立起来。
children.notes: with strong support from ... 介词短语作状语；was built upon 被建立在……之上；tireless tireless 的意思是“不知疲倦的”。
children.statement: tireless 里的 -less 是一个表否定意义的后缀。
children.explanation: tire (疲倦) + less (无……的) = 不知疲倦的，不懈的。
children.keywords: support, government, tireless efforts, built upon, ruins
children.highlight: With, from the, and the, of the, was built upon

children.text: Revival Achievements & Lessons (城市复苏成就与团结启示)
The new city has become a home to more than seven million people, with great improvements in transportation, industry, and environment.
children.cn: 这座新城市已经成为700多万人的家园，在交通、工业和环境方面取得了巨大的进步。
children.notes: has become 现在完成时；improvements in ... 在……方面的提升；million 后面指代人时不能加 s。
children.statement: million 后面有具体名词修饰，不用加 s，如 seven million people。
children.explanation: 具体数字修饰 million, billion 等词时，词尾不加 s，且直接修饰名词（不用 of）。
children.keywords: become home, seven million, improvements, transportation, industry
children.highlight: has become a, to more than, with great, in

children.text: Tangshan city has proved to China and the rest of the world that in times of disaster, people must unify and show the wisdom to stay positive and rebuild for a brighter future.
children.cn: 唐山市向中国及世界其他地方证明，在灾难时期，人们必须团结一致，表现出保持乐观、在废墟上重建更美好明天的智慧。
children.notes: proved to sb. that ... 向某人证明了……（that 引导宾语从句）；in times of disaster 在灾难时期；stay positive 保持乐观；unify 团结，统一。
children.statement: that 引导的是一个原因状语从句。
children.explanation: that 在此句中引导的是 proved 后的宾语从句，充当它的宾语成分。
children.keywords: proved, rest of world, unify, wisdom, stay positive, rebuild
children.highlight: has proved to, that in, people must, and show the, to stay, and rebuild for a

tree.notes: 杰克·伦敦关于1906年旧金山大地震和随之而来的火灾的目击叙事，展现了城市的毁灭和幸存者的团结高尚。
tree.statement: 本文通过目击者的视角，真实而生动地记录了旧金山地震以及火灾灾难。
tree.explanation: 文章以作者亲身经历叙述，描绘了城市毁灭的惨况以及人们互助的善举。

children.text: Paragraph 1: City Destroyed (城市毁灭)
children.cn: 第一段：城市彻底毁灭的现状
children.notes: 地震和火灾造成的惨重金钱与物资损失，城市几乎化为乌有。
children.statement: 本段通过金钱损失的对比指出了火灾的危害远大于地震本身。
children.explanation: 文中提到地震造成数十万美元的损失，但随后的火灾烧毁了数亿美元的房屋，且指出 San Francisco is gone。

children.text: Huge Property Loss (地震震塌与火灾烧毁的对比)
The earthquake that hit San Francisco on April 18 shook down hundreds of thousands of dollars' worth of walls and chimneys.
children.cn: 4月18日袭击旧金山的地震震塌了价值数十万美元的墙壁和烟囱。
children.notes: that hit San Francisco on April 18 是定语从句，修饰 earthquake；shook down 是 shake down（震塌，摇落）的过去式。
children.statement: shook 是 shake 的过去式形式。
children.explanation: 动词 shake 过去式为 shook，过去分词为 shaken。
children.keywords: earthquake, hit, San Francisco, shook down
children.highlight: that hit, on, shook down, worth of

children.text: But the fire that followed burned up hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of buildings and homes.
children.cn: 但随之而来的大火烧毁了价值数亿美元的建筑物和家园。
children.notes: that followed 定语从句修饰 fire；burned up 意为“烧毁，烧尽”。
children.statement: followed 在从句中是过去式，作谓语。
children.explanation: 关系代词 that 代表 fire 作主语，followed 是从句的动词过去式作谓语。
children.highlight: But the, that followed, burned up, worth of

children.text: City Completely Gone (城市各行各业的彻底毁灭)
Never before in history has a city been so completely destroyed.
children.cn: 历史上从未有一座城市被摧毁得如此彻底。
children.notes: 这是一个倒装句，由于否定副词 Never 放在句首，助动词 has 提前至主语前，构成部分倒装。
children.statement: 此句是一般现在时的倒装句。
children.explanation: 此句是现在完成时（has ... been destroyed）的倒装句，而不是一般现在时。
children.highlight: Never before, has a, been so

children.text: San Francisco is gone.
children.cn: 旧金山不复存在了。
children.notes: gone 是形容词/过去分词作表语，意为“消失了的，离去的”。
children.statement: gone 在这里表示“去到某地了”。
children.explanation: gone 在此表示“消失了的，毁灭了的”，形容旧金山已经被大火付之一炬的状态。
children.highlight: is gone

children.text: The factories, the great stores and newspaper buildings, the hotels, and the great houses of the rich are all gone.
children.cn: 工厂、大商铺、报社大楼、旅馆，以及富人们的豪宅，全部化为了乌有。
children.notes: the rich 意为“富人们”，由 the + 形容词构成复数名词代称；are all gone 均已消失。
children.statement: the rich 作主语时，谓语动词应该用单数形式。
children.explanation: “the + 形容词”表示一类人时，在句中作主语，谓语动词必须使用复数形式（这里用 are）。
children.highlight: The, and the, of the, are all gone

children.text: Paragraph 2: The Outbreak of Fire (火势蔓延)
children.cn: 第二段：火灾的爆发和不可阻挡
children.notes: 地震爆发的时刻，以及由于水管震裂导致消防工作完全无法开展的尴尬局面。
children.statement: 本段分析了火灾失控的技术原因：主要是因为水管破裂，消防员巧妇难为无水之炊。
children.explanation: 文中指出 water mains were useless and there was no water in water pipes，所以消防员无能为力。

children.text: Earthquake Outbreak & Fires (地震降临与大火初燃)
On Wednesday morning at a quarter past five came the earthquake.
children.cn: 星期三清晨五点一刻，地震降临了。
children.notes: 此句为完全倒装句，表时间的介词短语置于句首，动词 came 移到主语 the earthquake 前。
children.statement: a quarter past five 意思是五点四十五分。
children.explanation: a quarter past five 是 5点过15分（一刻钟），即5点15分。5点45分是 a quarter to six 或 five forty-five。
children.keywords: Wednesday, morning, quarter past five, came
children.highlight: On, at a, past, came the

children.text: A moment later, the disaster was a fact.
children.cn: 片刻之后，灾难已成事实。
children.notes: disaster was a fact 灾难成为事实，说明灾难破坏无可挽回。
children.statement: moment 意为“时刻，瞬间”。
children.explanation: a moment later 表示一会之后，片刻之后。
children.highlight: A, later, the, was a

children.text: South of Market Street, in the working-class neighbourhoods and in the factories, fires started.
children.cn: 在市场街以南的工薪阶层社区和工厂里，大火燃了起来。
children.notes: South of Market Street 市场街以南；working-class neighbourhoods 工薪阶层居民区。
children.statement: fires started 中的 started 是及物动词。
children.explanation: 这里的 start 是不及物动词，意为“大火开始燃烧起来”。
children.highlight: South of, in the, and in the, started

children.text: Spread & Smoke Plumes (火势蔓延与浓烟滚滚)
Within an hour of the first quake, the smoke could be seen 100 miles away.
children.cn: 在第一次地震发生后不到一小时，100英里外就能看见浓烟。
children.notes: could be seen 过去式情态动词被动语态；100 miles away 100英里之外。
children.statement: could be seen 表示浓烟可以被看见。
children.explanation: can/could be seen 是情态动词被动语态的常见形式。
children.highlight: Within an, of the, could be, away

children.text: The sun was red in the dark sky.
children.cn: 黑暗的天空中，太阳是红色的。
children.notes: sun 前必须加上定冠词 the，天空前也同样使用 the sky。
children.statement: dark 是副词，修饰 sky。
children.explanation: dark 在此处是形容词，修饰名词 sky，表示“黑暗的天空”。
children.highlight: The, was, in the

children.text: Firefighting Failure (消防水源断绝与防线崩溃)
There was no stopping the fires.
children.cn: 根本无法阻止火势蔓延。
children.notes: There is/was no doing sth. 是固定句型，表示“无法做某事”。
children.statement: no stopping the fires 意为“没有停止的大火”。
children.explanation: There was no stopping ... 表示“不可能阻止/停下……”。这是一种强调无法挽回的表达形式。
children.highlight: There was no, the

children.text: The firemen could do nothing because the water mains were now useless and there was no water in water pipes.
children.cn: 消防员们束手无策，因为主水管现在已经没用了，水管里根本没有水。
children.notes: because 引导原因状语从句；water mains 主水管；water pipes 水管。
children.statement: useless 是形容词，用作表语。
children.explanation: useless 跟在系动词 were 后面，作表语说明主水管的状态。
children.keywords: firemen, because, water mains, useless, pipes
children.highlight: could do, because the, were now, and there was no, in

children.text: All of the ways man had made to keep the city safe were gone in the 30 seconds the earth moved.
children.cn: 在地球晃动的短短30秒内，人类为了保护城市安全所创造的一切手段都烟消云散了。
children.notes: man had made to keep the city safe 是省略了关系代词 that/which 的定语从句，修饰 ways；the earth moved 是定语从句修饰 30 seconds。
children.statement: had made 是过去完成时，表示在地震（were gone）之前人类已经做好了的安全预防措施。
children.explanation: 定语从句中的动作“创造防御方式”发生在大火烧毁这一切（were gone）之前，故用过去完成时形式。
children.keywords: ways, city safe, gone, seconds, earth moved
children.highlight: All of the, man had, to keep the, were gone in the, the earth

children.text: Paragraph 3: Author's Observation (作者观察)
children.cn: 第三段：作者在海湾上的观察与异风
children.notes: 作者从海湾的船上观察灾情，描绘了海上风平浪静与陆地狂风肆虐的诡异对比。
children.statement: 本段指出了强风从四面八方向旧金山吹去，加速了火灾蔓延。
children.explanation: 文中吞食了“strong winds blew upon the unlucky city from every direction”。

children.text: City Center Destroyed (城市核心地带在午后被毁)
By Wednesday afternoon, half the heart of the city was gone.
children.cn: 到星期三下午，半个城市中心已经不复存在。
children.notes: By Wednesday afternoon 意为“到星期三下午为止”；the heart of the city 城市中心地带。
children.statement: gone 是 go 的过去式。
children.explanation: gone 是 go 的过去分词，在此处用作形容词作表语，意为“消失了的，不见了的”。
children.highlight: By, half the, of the, was gone

children.text: At that time, I watched the disaster from a ship on the bay.
children.cn: 那时，我站在湾区的一艘船上注视着这场灾难。
children.notes: watched 是 watch 的过去式；on the bay 在海湾上。
children.statement: disaster 前面使用了定冠词 the，特指上文提到的地震和火灾灾难。
children.explanation: the disaster 特指旧金山的大灾难，属于特指用法。
children.highlight: At that time, I, from a, on the

children.text: Observations from Bay (在平静海湾上的极目远眺)
Out at sea it was calm.
children.cn: 海上风平浪静。
children.notes: Out at sea 意为“在海面上，出海在外的洋面上”；calm 意为“平静的，无风浪的”。
children.statement: calm 在此是形容词，作表语说明海上天气特征。
children.explanation: calm 用于形容海洋、湖泊或气候时，表示没有强风浪，风平浪静。
children.highlight: Out at, it was

children.text: No wind came up.
children.cn: 没有一丝风刮起。
children.notes: came up 意为“产生，刮起（风等）”。
children.statement: No wind 表示没有任何风。
children.explanation: No 用于名词前表示否定，意为“没有，无”。
children.highlight: No wind, came up

children.text: Yet from every direction—east, west, north, and south—strong winds blew upon the unlucky city and those whose homes had once stood in its green hills.
children.cn: 然而，从东、西、南、北的每一个方向，狂风正吹向这座不幸的城市，也吹向那些家园曾立于绿色山丘之中的人们。
children.notes: whose homes had once stood ... 是定语从句，whose 引导定语从句表示所属关系；had once stood 过去完成时。
children.statement: blew 是 blow 的过去式形式。
children.explanation: blow 的变化为 blow - blew - blown，blew 是其过去式。
children.keywords: every direction, strong winds, blew, homes, green hills
children.highlight: Yet from, direction, strong winds, upon the, and those whose, had once stood in

children.text: Paragraph 4: Night of Destruction (废墟缔造)
children.cn: 第四段：深夜的彻底毁灭与主动拆楼
children.notes: 深夜的大火吞噬了核心城区，人们甚至不得不主动炸毁一些精美的大楼以制造隔离带阻断火源。
children.statement: 本段指出有时候人类必须自己动手炸毁精美建筑以在街道上防火并保持安全。
children.explanation: 文中提到“Man himself had to make ruins of some of the city's best buildings so that they would not be a danger”。

children.text: Very Heart Destruction (星期三夜里市中心的最终沦陷)
Wednesday night saw the destruction of the very heart of the city.
children.cn: 星期三晚上见证了这座城市最中心地带的毁灭。
children.notes: 本句使用了拟人手法，将时间 Wednesday night 充当主语，谓语动词用 saw；the very heart 最中心（very 起强调作用）。
children.statement: destruction 是 destroy 的名词形式。
children.explanation: 动词 destroy 转化为名词时为不规则变化，即 destruction。
children.keywords: Wednesday night, saw, destruction, very heart
children.highlight: night saw the, of the, very heart of the

children.text: Demolishing Best Buildings (炸毁好楼以造隔离防火带)
Man himself had to make ruins of some of the city's best buildings so that they would not be a danger to those in the streets.
children.cn: 人类自己不得不将一些城市最好的大楼夷为废墟，以防止它们对街上的人造成危险。
children.notes: had to 不得不；so that 引导目的状语从句，意为“以便，为了”。
children.statement: so that 引导的是一个结果状语从句。
children.explanation: 此处的 so that 意为“为了，以便于”，引导的是目的状语从句，说明把楼房化为废墟的目的是为了防止对街上的人造成危险。
children.keywords: himself, make ruins, best buildings, so that, danger, streets
children.highlight: had to, make ruins of, so that they, would not be a, to those in

children.text: Paragraph 5: Spirit of Survivors (人性的光辉)
children.cn: 第五段：灾民的疏散以及人性尊严的闪耀
children.notes: 数万难民被迫离开旧金山，但灾难没有引发混乱，反而展现了极高的秩序、友善与人道精神。
children.statement: 本段的主旨是灾难无情人有情，赞美了旧金山居民在恐慌之夜表现出的极度善良。
children.explanation: 文章结尾段写到“this worst of disasters brought out the best in the survivors”以及人们的慷慨友善。

children.text: Evacuating Refugees (千万失去家园者的秩序疏散)
Tens of thousands who had lost their homes left the city to look for shelter from the fires.
children.cn: 成千上万失去家园的人离开了城市，去寻找能躲避大火的避难所。
children.notes: who had lost their homes 是定语从句，修饰 Tens of thousands；to look for 是不定式短语作目的状语。
children.statement: had lost 是过去完成时，表示“失去家园”动作发生在“离开城市”（left）之前。
children.explanation: 失去房屋发生在离开城市去寻找避难所之前，符合“过去的过去”，使用过去完成时。
children.keywords: tens of thousands, lost homes, left city, look for shelter
children.highlight: who had, lost their, left the, to look for, from the

children.text: Some were dressed only in blankets and carried the things that they had been able to rescue from the fires.
children.cn: 有的人身上只裹着毛毯，手里提着从大火中抢救出来的东西。
children.notes: were dressed in 穿着，裹着；that they had been able to rescue... 是定语从句修饰 things。
children.statement: blankets 意为“被子，毯子”。
children.explanation: blanket 意为“毛毯，毯子”。
children.highlight: were dressed only, in, and carried the, that they had been, to rescue from

children.text: Kindness & Solidarity (恐怖之夜里最善良的一面)
But there were no fights and no pushing or shoving.
children.cn: 但是没有任何打斗，也没有任何推搡或挤撞。
children.notes: no pushing or shoving 意为“没有推搡或推挤”；there were 表示过去的存在状态。
children.statement: shoving 和 pushing 在此句中是动名词形式作并列主语。
children.explanation: pushing and shoving 在这里用作动名词表示推搡和推挤的动作，与 fights 并列作句子的主语成分。
children.highlight: But there were, and no, or

children.text: Somehow this worst of disasters brought out the best in the survivors.
children.cn: 不知何故，这场最坏的灾难却激发了幸存者身上最好的一面。
children.notes: Somehow 意为“不知怎么地，以某种方式”；brought out 激发，带出。
children.statement: worst 是 bad 的最高级形式。
children.explanation: 形容词 bad / ill 的比较级是 worse，最高级是 worst。
children.keywords: somehow, worst disasters, brought out, best, survivors
children.highlight: Somehow this, of disasters, brought out the, in the

children.text: Never in all of San Francisco's history were her people so kind as on this night of terror.
children.cn: 在旧金山的全部历史上，她的人民从未像这个恐怖之夜这样善良。
children.notes: 这是一个倒装句，否定副词 Never 位于句首引起部分倒装，把系动词 were 提到了主语 her people 之前。
children.statement: were her people so kind as on 中的 her 指代旧金山这座城市。
children.explanation: 在英语修辞中，常将城市或国家拟人化为女性，用 her / she 指代。
children.keywords: history, people, kind, night of terror
children.highlight: Never in, of, were her, so kind as, on this, of terror
